name: Pat Zanchelli Inc
recent_news: Long-running small family contractor; CEO Shirley Zanchelli reportedly ~85 years old per public records, suggesting near-term succession need.
fit_notes: Tiny single-yard NY operator with elderly principal and clear succession angle; ~19 buses serving Hudson CSD. Too small for standalone platform value but a clean tuck-in for a NY/Hudson Valley density play.

name: A M & C/Bus Resource Group
recent_news: No public web presence found; address is in Atlanta industrial park (3601 Southside Ind Ct SE). Could not confirm as an active school-bus contractor versus a bus dealer/repair/broker.
fit_notes: Insufficient evidence this is an operating school-bus contractor; may be a dealer or resource/broker entity rather than a fleet operator. Needs direct phone diligence before treating as a tuck-in candidate.

name: Consolidated Bus Co
recent_news: Privately/family-owned NYC operator >50 years in business, transports 25,000+ kids twice daily; multi-borough yards in Bronx and Brooklyn.
fit_notes: The Bronx address (191 Bruckner Blvd) is one yard of the much larger Consolidated Bus Transit family-owned NYC platform (CBT). This is a top-tier NYC OPT contractor, not a single-yard tuck-in - the realistic deal is a whole-company acquisition of CBT, which would be a major NYC platform add for Summit if Genovese/Mancuso families ever sell.

name: Scholastic School Bus
recent_news: NYC-certified Woman-Owned Small Business with 100+ bus fleet; ~29 years in operation; offices in Brooklyn (8024 Preston Ct) and East Stroudsburg PA.
fit_notes: Scholastic Transportation is a credible mid-sized NYC/Tri-State operator (~100 buses, WBE-certified) - the listed 1188 E 42nd St Brooklyn address is the operator's neighborhood; underlying entity is Scholastic Transportation. Decent NYC tuck-in candidate; private family ownership with WBE certification adds diligence complexity but also DOE contract continuity value.

name: Missouri Central School Bus
owner_or_parent: North America Central School Bus (NACSB) - H.I.G. Capital (majority) and Gryphon Investors
districts_or_customers: formerly St. Louis Public Schools (contract terminated June 2024)
recent_news: March 2024: Missouri Central terminated SLPS contract a year early and closed its 3645 Gratiot St. and 8000 Hall St. St. Louis facilities, laying off ~300-332 workers after driver shortages and workplace incidents. Parent NACSB is owned by H.I.G. Capital (acquired Aug 2021 from Goldner Hawn) with Gryphon Junior Capital co-investment.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target - subsidiary of PE-owned NACSB (H.I.G. Capital + Gryphon), Summit's direct strategic competitor (~6,200 vehicles, 945k students). Missouri footprint has shrunk after SLPS contract loss. Only relevant as a corporate carve-out or sector intel; reputational baggage from noose incident and worker walkouts.
